tools.name: Resume Screening Tool
tools.tagline: Score a resume against any job description in seconds — with reasons you can read.
tools.description: Paste or upload a job description and a resume, and HireSort builds a screening rubric on the fly, then grades the resume against it. You get an overall fit score, a criterion-by-criterion breakdown, and a plain-English explanation for every point — no black box, no guessing. It's the same explainable engine that powers HireSort, available free with no signup.
whenToUse: You have a single resume and want a fast, structured read before an interview.
You want to see how AI screening scores a candidate before rolling it out to your team.
You need an objective second opinion to sanity-check a gut-feel shortlist decision.
You're hiring solo and don't have a recruiter or ATS to lean on.

related.label: Resume screening checklist
related.note: What to look for, step by step

tools.name: Rubric Generator
tools.tagline: Turn a job description into a weighted, ready-to-score screening rubric.
tools.description: Drop in a job description and the Rubric Generator drafts a structured screening rubric for you — must-have requirements, weighted criteria, and clear scoring bands — so every resume and every interviewer is judged against the same bar. Edit it, export it, and reuse it across the whole pipeline. Launching soon; in the meantime, our role-based rubric templates cover the most common positions.
whenToUse: You're standing up a structured hiring process and need a defensible scoring framework.
You want every reviewer evaluating candidates against the same weighted criteria.
You're tired of writing rubrics from scratch for each new role.
You need an audit-friendly, explainable basis for screening decisions.

related.label: Rubric template guide
related.note: How to build one that holds up

related.label: Interview questions by role
related.note: Pair criteria with the right questions

tools.name: Job Description Generator
tools.tagline: Turn a role and a few details into a clear, structured job description — written by AI.
tools.description: Give the Job Description Generator a role title and a few specifics — seniority, must-have skills, team, and location — and it drafts a complete, well-structured job description you can publish or refine. Consistent sections, inclusive language, and the right level of detail every time, so you spend minutes instead of hours on every new opening. Launching soon; in the meantime, our role-based JD templates cover the most common positions.
whenToUse: You're opening a new role and need a polished JD fast.
You want consistent structure and tone across every job posting.
You're starting from a blank page and want a strong first draft.
You need a JD that maps cleanly to a screening rubric later.

related.label: Job description templates
related.note: Role-ready JDs to copy and customize

related.label: Interview questions by role
related.note: Pair the JD with the right questions

related.label: Screening rubrics
related.note: Turn the JD into weighted criteria

tools.name: Hiring Analytics Spreadsheet
tools.tagline: Download a recruiting dashboard workbook for requisitions, pipeline stages, interviews, and bottleneck alerts.
tools.description: Use this free Excel workbook to track open requisitions, candidate movement, interview activity, source performance, recruiter workload, stale candidates, pending offers, and hiring bottlenecks. It includes a dashboard plus structured tabs for requisitions, candidate pipeline, today's interviews, reference data, and recommendations.
whenToUse: You manage several open roles and need one recruiting dashboard for weekly review.
You want to measure hiring funnel conversion without building a BI dashboard from scratch.
Your ATS does not give recruiters and hiring managers an easy operational view.
You need a simple candidate pipeline tracker for small teams, agencies, or startup hiring.

related.label: Hiring analytics guide
related.note: How to use the workbook efficiently
